title: Bahelia angustifolia
intro: Bahelia angustifolia, also known as Baheda, is a type of deciduous tree native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Anacardiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Bahelia angustifolia include flavonoids, terpenes, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Bahelia angustifolia is commonly used to prepare herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, often in combination with other herbs like Triphala, to support digestive health and overall well-being.
taxonomy: Bahelia angustifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Asia. The classification of Bahelia angustifolia is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. Bahelia angustifolia belongs to the order Magnoliales and the family Annonaceae. Its classification is a key factor in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of this plant species.
uses: Bahelia angustifolia is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Its bark, leaves, and roots are used to make medicinal preparations. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In folk medicine, it is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Bahelia angustifolia's medicinal uses.
